 {"id":"xf-11.2.5","title":"Add tests for enhanced stats analytics","description":"## Goal

Comprehensive test coverage for the enhanced stats analytics feature.

## Unit Tests (stats_analytics.rs)

### Temporal Analytics Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_sparkline_generation() {
    let values = vec![1, 5, 10, 8, 3, 1];
    let spark = sparkline(&values, 6);
    assert_eq!(spark.chars().count(), 6);
    // Highest value (10) should be █
    assert!(spark.contains('█'));
}

#[test]
fn test_sparkline_empty_input() {
    let values: Vec<u64> = vec![];
    let spark = sparkline(&values, 10);
    assert_eq!(spark, "");
}

#[test]
fn test_sparkline_single_value() {
    let values = vec![5];
    let spark = sparkline(&values, 1);
    assert_eq!(spark, "█");  // Single value is max
}

#[test]
fn test_gap_detection_normal() {
    let counts = vec![
        (NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 1), 5),
        (NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 5), 3),  // 4 day gap
        (NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 20), 2), // 15 day gap (longest)
        (NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 22), 1), // 2 day gap
    ];
    let (gap, start, end) = find_longest_gap(&counts);
    assert_eq!(gap.num_days(), 15);
    assert_eq!(start, NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 5));
}

#[test]
fn test_gap_detection_single_day() {
    let counts = vec![(NaiveDate::from_ymd(2023, 1, 1), 5)];
    let (gap, _, _) = find_longest_gap(&counts);
    assert_eq!(gap.num_days(), 0);
}

#[test]
fn test_hourly_distribution() {
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_tweets(vec![
        ("tweet1", "2023-01-01T09:00:00Z"),
        ("tweet2", "2023-01-01T09:30:00Z"),
        ("tweet3", "2023-01-01T21:00:00Z"),
    ]);
    let stats = compute_temporal_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stats.hourly_distribution[9], 2);  // 9 AM
    assert_eq!(stats.hourly_distribution[21], 1); // 9 PM
}
```

### Engagement Analytics Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_likes_histogram_buckets() {
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_engagement(vec![
        (0, 0), (1, 0), (3, 0), (5, 0),  // 4 in 0-5 bucket
        (10, 0), (15, 0),                // 2 in 6-25 bucket
        (100, 0),                        // 1 in 51-100 bucket
    ]);
    let stats = compute_engagement_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    // Verify bucket counts
}

#[test]
fn test_top_tweets_ordering() {
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_engagement(vec![
        (10, 5),   // total 15
        (100, 20), // total 120 (should be first)
        (50, 10),  // total 60
    ]);
    let stats = compute_engagement_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stats.top_tweets[0].total_engagement, 120);
}

#[test]
fn test_engagement_with_nulls() {
    // Tweets with NULL favorite_count should be handled gracefully
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_null_engagement();
    let stats = compute_engagement_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    // Should not panic, should skip nulls
}
```

### Content Analytics Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_hashtag_extraction() {
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_tweets(vec![
        "Hello #rust #programming",
        "More #rust content",
        "#Tech news",
    ]);
    let stats = compute_content_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stats.top_hashtags[0], ("rust".to_string(), 2));
}

#[test]
fn test_media_ratio() {
    let storage = create_test_storage_mixed_media(3, 7); // 3 with media, 7 without
    let stats = compute_content_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert!((stats.media_ratio - 0.3).abs() < 0.01);
}

#[test]
fn test_thread_detection() {
    // Create tweets where some reply to self
    let storage = create_test_storage_with_threads();
    let stats = compute_content_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stats.thread_count, expected_thread_count);
}
```

## Integration Tests

### Test: detailed_flag_produces_all_sections

```rust
#[test]
fn test_detailed_flag_text_output() {
    let output = run_xf(&["stats", "--detailed"]);
    assert!(output.contains("TEMPORAL PATTERNS"));
    assert!(output.contains("ENGAGEMENT"));
    assert!(output.contains("CONTENT"));
}
```

### Test: detailed_json_schema

```rust
#[test]
fn test_detailed_flag_json_output() {
    let output = run_xf(&["stats", "--detailed", "--format", "json"]);
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
    assert!(json["temporal"].is_object());
    assert!(json["engagement"].is_object());
    assert!(json["content"].is_object());
}
```

## Edge Case Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_empty_archive() {
    let storage = create_empty_storage();
    let stats = compute_temporal_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert!(stats.daily_counts.is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_single_tweet_archive() {
    // Edge case: archive with exactly one tweet
}

#[test]
fn test_massive_archive_performance() {
    // Generate 100k fake tweets, verify < 2s execution
    let storage = create_large_test_storage(100_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    let _ = compute_temporal_stats(&storage).unwrap();
    assert!(start.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(2));
}
```

## Logging Requirements

- Each test should log setup and teardown with tracing::debug!
- On assertion failure, log full computed state
- Log timing for performance-sensitive tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Edge cases covered (empty, single item, nulls)
- [ ] Performance test validates < 2s on 100k tweets
- [ ] Integration tests verify CLI output
- [ ] Test coverage > 80% for new code","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFox","created_at":"2026-01-10T19:08:23.225389-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:40:05.421163932-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:40:05.421163932-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-11.2.5","depends_on_id":"xf-11.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"issue_id":"xf-11.2.5","depends_on_id":"xf-11.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-11.2.6","title":"Create E2E test script for stats --detailed","description":"## Goal

Create a **reliable E2E script** for `xf stats --detailed` that validates output sections, JSON schema, and performance with detailed logging.

## Script: tests/e2e/stats_detailed_test.sh

### Setup
- Use a **known fixture** archive/DB under `tests/fixtures/` (or a small synthetic dataset built by a helper) to keep runtime deterministic.
- Respect `XF_DB` / `XF_INDEX` overrides if set.

### Checks
- Text output contains all required sections: Temporal, Engagement, Content.
- JSON output is valid and contains `temporal`, `engagement`, `content` objects.
- Exit code is 0 on healthy data.
- Performance: log duration; warn if >2s on fixture (do not hard‑fail unless agreed).

### Logging (must be verbose)
- Timestamped entries for each test.
- Capture command, stdout, stderr, exit code, and duration.
- On failure, print the failing output snippet and context.

## Integration Touchpoints

- Script should be runnable in CI and locally with no network access.
- Use `set -euo pipefail` and explicit trap to report final summary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] E2E script added and executable
- [ ] Validates text + JSON output for `--detailed`
- [ ] Logs command/stdout/stderr/exit code/duration
- [ ] Uses deterministic fixture data
- [ ] Non‑zero exit on failure with actionable logs

 {"id":"xf-11.3","title":"Interactive REPL Mode (xf shell)","description":"## Overview

Interactive REPL (`xf shell`) for iterative searches, refinements, and exports with history + completion.

## Key Capabilities

- Fast loop for `search`, `stats`, `list`, `refine`, `show`, `export`.
- Session state with pagination and variable references.
- Tab completion for commands/flags/values.
- History persistence (opt‑out).

## Test Strategy

- Unit tests for parsing, session state, completion.
- Integration tests for REPL flow with test DB/index.
- E2E script exercising non‑interactive REPL sessions with detailed logging.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf shell` launches REPL and accepts commands
- [ ] State, pagination, and variable substitution work
- [ ] Completion works and is safe
- [ ] History persists when enabled
- [ ] Full test coverage with logs

-{"id":"xf-11.3.1","title":"Implement REPL core with rustyline","description":"## Goal

Implement the **core REPL loop** using `rustyline`, providing stable command parsing, history, and error handling that mirrors CLI behavior.

## Functional Requirements

- `xf shell` launches a REPL that supports: `search`, `stats`, `list`, `refine`, `more`, `show`, `export`, `help`, `quit` (+ aliases).
- Ctrl+C resets the prompt (no exit). Ctrl+D exits cleanly.
- History file is loaded/saved when enabled; skip history for `quit/exit` commands.
- Prompt context shows result count or DM context when relevant.
- No network access; all data stays local.

## Implementation Notes

- Use `rustyline` with Emacs editing, history dedupe, and list completion mode (actual completer added in xf-11.3.2).
- Keep parsing logic isolated and reusable for tests.
- Avoid panics on empty/whitespace input; provide friendly errors for unknown commands.

## Tests

### Unit
- Parsing for each command + alias.
- Prompt formatting for normal/results/DM contexts.
- History path resolution (respect `--history-file`, `--no-history`).
- Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D handling (simulate via helper functions).

### Integration
- Non‑interactive session (`printf "help\nquit\n" | xf shell`) exits 0.
- History file created only when enabled.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_smoke_test.sh)
- Runs a basic REPL session and validates output + exit code.
- Logs timestamp, command, stdout/stderr, exit code, duration.

## Logging

- `info!` on REPL start/exit.
- `debug!` on command parse/execute.
- `warn!` on unknown commands or execution errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] REPL loop runs with proper Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D behavior
- [ ] History loads/saves when enabled and is skipped when disabled
- [ ] Commands parsed + dispatched without panics
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs
","notes":"Started REPL core with rustyline: added dependency, new src/repl.rs with run loop, prompt context, search/stats/help/quit parsing, history support, logging, and basic result printing. +{"id":"xf-11.3.1","title":"Implement REPL core with rustyline","description":"## Goal

Implement the **core REPL loop** using `rustyline`, providing stable command parsing, history, and error handling that mirrors CLI behavior.

## Functional Requirements

- `xf shell` launches a REPL that supports: `search`, `stats`, `list`, `refine`, `more`, `show`, `export`, `help`, `quit` (+ aliases).
- Ctrl+C resets the prompt (no exit). Ctrl+D exits cleanly.
- History file is loaded/saved when enabled; skip history for `quit/exit` commands.
- Prompt context shows result count or DM context when relevant.
- No network access; all data stays local.

## Implementation Notes

- Use `rustyline` with Emacs editing, history dedupe, and list completion mode (actual completer added in xf-11.3.2).
- Keep parsing logic isolated and reusable for tests.
- Avoid panics on empty/whitespace input; provide friendly errors for unknown commands.

## Tests

### Unit
- Parsing for each command + alias.
- Prompt formatting for normal/results/DM contexts.
- History path resolution (respect `--history-file`, `--no-history`).
- Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D handling (simulate via helper functions).

### Integration
- Non‑interactive session (`printf "help\nquit\n" | xf shell`) exits 0.
- History file created only when enabled.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_smoke_test.sh)
- Runs a basic REPL session and validates output + exit code.
- Logs timestamp, command, stdout/stderr, exit code, duration.

## Logging

- `info!` on REPL start/exit.
- `debug!` on command parse/execute.
- `warn!` on unknown commands or execution errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] REPL loop runs with proper Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D behavior
- [ ] History loads/saves when enabled and is skipped when disabled
- [ ] Commands parsed + dispatched without panics
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs

 {"id":"xf-11.3.2","title":"Add tab completion for REPL commands","description":"## Goal

Add robust tab completion for REPL commands, flags, and context‑aware values with safe handling of quotes/comma lists.

## Completion Rules

- **Command position**: complete commands + aliases.
- **Flag position**: flags for active command.
- **Value position**:
  - `--types` → dm/tweet/like/grok/follower/following/block/mute
  - `--format` → text/json/json-pretty/csv/compact
  - `list` target → tweets/likes/dms/conversations/followers/following/blocks/mutes/files
- Handle `--flag value`, `--flag=value`, and comma‑separated values (`--types dm,li`).
- If cursor is inside quotes, **do not** attempt command/flag completion (avoid corrupting user input).

## Implementation Notes

- Use `rustyline::completion::Completer` with `Pair { display, replacement }`.
- Keep completions deterministic, sorted, and free of duplicates.
- `extract_word_at_position` must handle `=` and commas without panics.

## Tests

### Unit
- Command completions for partial input.
- Flag completions per command (`search`, `list`, `stats`, `export`, `refine`).
- Value completions for `--types`, `--format`, `list` target.
- Comma‑separated completion (e.g., `--types dm,li` suggests `like`).
- No completions when cursor is inside quotes.

### Integration
- REPL with completer enabled; tab on empty line does not crash.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_completion_test.sh)
- Non‑interactive session that triggers completion (e.g., via `RUSTYLINE` env or scripted input if supported).
- Validates that a completion suggestion list appears for `se<Tab>` and `--format <Tab>`.
- Logs command/stdout/stderr/exit code/duration.

## Logging

- `trace!` when computing completion context.
- `debug!` for candidate counts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command/flag/value completions work
- [ ] Handles quotes and comma lists safely
- [ ] Deterministic, sorted suggestions
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs

 {"id":"xf-11.3.3","title":"Add REPL session state and query refinement","description":"## Goal

Add REPL session state for cached results, query refinement, pagination, and variable substitution while keeping behavior consistent with CLI filtering semantics.

## Behavior

- Cache `last_results`, `last_query`, `current_offset`, and `page_size` after each search.
- `refine` filters cached results using the **same rules** as CLI (types, since/until, replies/no-replies).
- `more` paginates using `page_size`; prints “no more results” when exhausted.
- Variable refs:
  - `$1`, `$2` etc = Nth result
  - `$_` = last selected
  - `$*` = all result IDs
  - `$name` = named variables (`set $name value`)
- Pipes (`cmd1 | cmd2`) execute sequentially; each command can mutate state.

## Implementation Notes

- Reuse CLI formatting/output helpers where possible.
- For date filters in `refine`, reuse `date_parser::parse_date_flexible` for parity.
- Avoid cloning large result sets unless needed; prefer indices or references.

## Tests

### Unit
- `refine` reduces result set for date/type filters.
- `more` advances pagination; handles end‑of‑results gracefully.
- Variable resolution for `$1`, `$_`, `$*`, `$name`.
- Pipe execution order is preserved.

### Integration
- Simulated REPL flow: `search` → `refine` → `more` → `show`.
- `refine` yields same results as running a new CLI search with same filters.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_state_test.sh)
- Runs a scripted REPL session verifying `refine` + `more` + variable selection.
- Logs command/stdout/stderr/exit code/duration.

## Logging

- `debug!` on state transitions (results count, offset).
- `warn!` on invalid references or empty cache.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cached results and pagination behave deterministically
- [ ] `refine` matches CLI semantics
- [ ] Variable substitution works for all forms
- [ ] Pipes execute sequentially and mutate state correctly
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs

-{"id":"xf-11.3.4","title":"Add xf shell subcommand to CLI","description":"## Goal

Expose `xf shell` CLI subcommand that launches the REPL with configurable prompt, pagination, and history settings.

## CLI Contract

- `xf shell` uses global `--db` / `--index` overrides.
- Options:
  - `--prompt <str>` (default: `xf> `)
  - `--page-size <n>` (default: 10)
  - `--no-history`
  - `--history-file <path>` (overrides default `~/.xf_history`)

## Behavior

- Fails with a clear error if DB/index not found.
- History writes are disabled when `--no-history` is set.
- Prompt reflects `--prompt` in REPL output (including multi-word prompts).

## Tests

### Unit
- clap parsing for `shell` and its options.

### Integration
- `xf shell --help` contains examples and options.
- With `--no-history`, no history file is created.
- With `--history-file`, history is written to that path.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/shell_cli.sh)
- Starts REPL non-interactively (`echo "quit" | xf shell`) and validates exit code 0.
- Verifies prompt string appears in output.
- Logs command, output, and duration.

## Logging

- `info!` when shell starts and exits.
- `debug!` with resolved config (prompt/page_size/history_path).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf shell` launches REPL and exits cleanly
- [ ] All shell flags work as documented
- [ ] Help text and examples are accurate
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs

-{"id":"xf-11.3.5","title":"Add tests for REPL mode","description":"## Goal

Comprehensive test coverage for REPL mode including unit, integration, and E2E validation with **deterministic** history paths and verbose logs.

## Unit Tests (repl.rs)

- Command parsing (search/stats/list/refine/more/show/export/help/quit + aliases).
- Flag parsing inside REPL (types, format, limit, since/until).
- Prompt formatting for each context.
- Variable resolution ($1, $_, $* and named variables).
- `refine` + `more` pagination behavior.

## Integration Tests

- Simulate REPL flow with a test storage/index (search → refine → more → show).
- History persistence uses a **temp dir** (no writes to user home).
- Ensure `--no-history` disables file writes.

## E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_test.sh)

- Run non‑interactive sessions (`printf "search ...\nquit\n" | xf shell`).
- Validate:
  - REPL starts and exits cleanly.
  - Help output contains key commands.
  - Unknown command yields error.
  - History file created only when enabled.
- Logs: timestamp, command, stdout/stderr, exit code, and duration.

## Logging Requirements

- `trace!` for parse decisions and completion contexts.
- `debug!` for state transitions (result count, offset).
- `warn!` for invalid refs and command errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests cover parsing, state, variables, pagination
- [ ] Integration tests validate session flow and history
- [ ] E2E script validates interactive behavior
- [ ] Deterministic temp history paths (no user‑home writes)
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test`

-{"id":"xf-11.4","title":"xf doctor Health Check Command","description":"## Overview

Add `xf doctor` to diagnose archive, database, index, and performance issues with **actionable fixes** and **stable machine-readable output**.

## User Value

- Explains missing data, slow search, and corruption symptoms.
- Provides safe, local fixes (no network access).
- Builds trust in the archive/index integrity.

## Scope

### Archive Checks
- Required files present
- JS-wrapped JSON structure validity
- Duplicate IDs
- Timestamp consistency

### Database Checks
- `PRAGMA integrity_check`
- FTS5 integrity + orphan detection
- Table counts vs. index counts

### Index Checks
- Tantivy version compatibility
- Segment count / doc count
- Sample query latency

### Performance Checks
- Index load time
- Simple/complex query latency
- FTS5 query latency

## Output Requirements

- Text: sectioned, colored, emoji status, summary + suggestions.
- JSON: stable schema (checks, summary, suggestions, runtime_ms).
- Exit codes: 0 pass/warn, 1 error, 2 critical.

## Safety

- `--fix` must be **safe and idempotent** only; never delete user data.
- All operations remain local (privacy-first).

## Test Strategy

### Unit
- Check logic for each category with deterministic fixtures.
- JSON schema serialization and summary counts.

### Integration
- CLI output for text/json formats.
- Exit codes for pass/warn/error/critical.

### E2E
- `tests/e2e/doctor_cli.sh` against healthy and intentionally broken archives.
- Logs command, timing, exit codes, and verifies output sections.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf doctor` runs all checks in <10s on large archives
- [ ] JSON output stable and validated
- [ ] Safe `--fix` reduces warnings without data loss
- [ ] Comprehensive tests + E2E with detailed logs

+{"id":"xf-11.3.4","title":"Add xf shell subcommand to CLI","description":"## Goal

Expose `xf shell` CLI subcommand that launches the REPL with configurable prompt, pagination, and history settings.

## CLI Contract

- `xf shell` uses global `--db` / `--index` overrides.
- Options:
  - `--prompt <str>` (default: `xf> `)
  - `--page-size <n>` (default: 10)
  - `--no-history`
  - `--history-file <path>` (overrides default `~/.xf_history`)

## Behavior

- Fails with a clear error if DB/index not found.
- History writes are disabled when `--no-history` is set.
- Prompt reflects `--prompt` in REPL output (including multi-word prompts).

## Tests

### Unit
- clap parsing for `shell` and its options.

### Integration
- `xf shell --help` contains examples and options.
- With `--no-history`, no history file is created.
- With `--history-file`, history is written to that path.

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/shell_cli.sh)
- Starts REPL non-interactively (`echo "quit" | xf shell`) and validates exit code 0.
- Verifies prompt string appears in output.
- Logs command, output, and duration.

## Logging

- `info!` when shell starts and exits.
- `debug!` with resolved config (prompt/page_size/history_path).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf shell` launches REPL and exits cleanly
- [ ] All shell flags work as documented
- [ ] Help text and examples are accurate
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs

+{"id":"xf-11.3.5","title":"Add tests for REPL mode","description":"## Goal

Comprehensive test coverage for REPL mode including unit, integration, and E2E validation with **deterministic** history paths and verbose logs.

## Unit Tests (repl.rs)

- Command parsing (search/stats/list/refine/more/show/export/help/quit + aliases).
- Flag parsing inside REPL (types, format, limit, since/until).
- Prompt formatting for each context.
- Variable resolution ($1, $_, $* and named variables).
- `refine` + `more` pagination behavior.

## Integration Tests

- Simulate REPL flow with a test storage/index (search → refine → more → show).
- History persistence uses a **temp dir** (no writes to user home).
- Ensure `--no-history` disables file writes.

## E2E Script (tests/e2e/repl_test.sh)

- Run non‑interactive sessions (`printf "search ...\nquit\n" | xf shell`).
- Validate:
  - REPL starts and exits cleanly.
  - Help output contains key commands.
  - Unknown command yields error.
  - History file created only when enabled.
- Logs: timestamp, command, stdout/stderr, exit code, and duration.

## Logging Requirements

- `trace!` for parse decisions and completion contexts.
- `debug!` for state transitions (result count, offset).
- `warn!` for invalid refs and command errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests cover parsing, state, variables, pagination
- [ ] Integration tests validate session flow and history
- [ ] E2E script validates interactive behavior
- [ ] Deterministic temp history paths (no user‑home writes)
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test`

+{"id":"xf-11.4","title":"xf doctor Health Check Command","description":"## Overview

Add `xf doctor` to diagnose archive, database, index, and performance issues with **actionable fixes** and **stable machine-readable output**.

## User Value

- Explains missing data, slow search, and corruption symptoms.
- Provides safe, local fixes (no network access).
- Builds trust in the archive/index integrity.

## Scope

### Archive Checks
- Required files present
- JS-wrapped JSON structure validity
- Duplicate IDs
- Timestamp consistency

### Database Checks
- `PRAGMA integrity_check`
- FTS5 integrity + orphan detection
- Table counts vs. index counts

### Index Checks
- Tantivy version compatibility
- Segment count / doc count
- Sample query latency

### Performance Checks
- Index load time
- Simple/complex query latency
- FTS5 query latency

## Output Requirements

- Text: sectioned, colored, emoji status, summary + suggestions.
- JSON: stable schema (checks, summary, suggestions, runtime_ms).
- Exit codes: 0 pass/warn, 1 error, 2 critical.

## Safety

- `--fix` must be **safe and idempotent** only; never delete user data.
- All operations remain local (privacy-first).

## Test Strategy

### Unit
- Check logic for each category with deterministic fixtures.
- JSON schema serialization and summary counts.

### Integration
- CLI output for text/json formats.
- Exit codes for pass/warn/error/critical.

### E2E
- `tests/e2e/doctor_cli.sh` against healthy and intentionally broken archives.
- Logs command, timing, exit codes, and verifies output sections.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf doctor` runs all checks in <10s on large archives
- [ ] JSON output stable and validated
- [ ] Safe `--fix` reduces warnings without data loss
- [ ] Comprehensive tests + E2E with detailed logs

 {"id":"xf-11.4.1","title":"Implement archive structure validation","description":"## Goal

Implement archive structure validation to verify the source archive is correctly formatted and complete.

## Checks to Implement

### 1. Required Files Presence

```rust
fn check_required_files(archive_path: &Path) -> Vec<HealthCheck> {
    let required = [
        ("data/tweets.js", true),
        ("data/tweets-part*.js", true),  // or parts
        ("data/direct-messages.js", false),
        ("data/direct-messages-group*.js", false),
        ("data/like.js", false),
        ("data/follower.js", false),
        ("data/following.js", false),
        ("data/block.js", false),
        ("data/mute.js", false),
    ];
    
    let mut checks = Vec::new();
    for (pattern, is_required) in required {
        let exists = glob_matches_any(archive_path, pattern);
        checks.push(HealthCheck {
            category: CheckCategory::Archive,
            name: format!("File: {}", pattern),
            status: if exists { 
                CheckStatus::Pass 
            } else if is_required {
                CheckStatus::Error
            } else {
                CheckStatus::Warning
            },
            message: if exists { 
                "Found".into() 
            } else { 
                "Not found".into() 
            },
            suggestion: if !exists && is_required {
                Some("Ensure archive was fully extracted".into())
            } else {
                None
            },
        });
    }
    checks
}
```

### 2. JSON Structure Validation

```rust
fn check_json_structure(archive_path: &Path) -> Vec<HealthCheck> {
    let files = ["tweets.js", "direct-messages.js", "like.js"];
    let mut checks = Vec::new();
    
    for file in files {
        let path = archive_path.join("data").join(file);
        if !path.exists() {
            continue;
        }
        
        match validate_js_wrapped_json(&path) {
            Ok((count, warnings)) => {
                checks.push(HealthCheck {
                    category: CheckCategory::Archive,
                    name: format!("Parse: {}", file),
                    status: if warnings.is_empty() { CheckStatus::Pass } else { CheckStatus::Warning },
                    message: format!("{} items parsed", count),
                    suggestion: warnings.first().cloned(),
                });
            }
            Err(e) => {
                checks.push(HealthCheck {
                    category: CheckCategory::Archive,
                    name: format!("Parse: {}", file),
                    status: CheckStatus::Error,
                    message: format!("Parse error: {}", e),
                    suggestion: Some("Check file is not corrupted".into()),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    checks
}

fn validate_js_wrapped_json(path: &Path) -> Result<(usize, Vec<String>)> {
    let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    
    // Strip JS wrapper: window.YTD.tweets.part0 = [...]
    let json_start = content.find('[')
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("No JSON array found"))?;
    let json = &content[json_start..];
    
    // Parse and count
    let items: Vec<Value> = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
    let mut warnings = Vec::new();
    
    // Check for common issues
    for (i, item) in items.iter().enumerate() {
        if item["tweet"]["id_str"].is_null() {
            warnings.push(format!("Item {} missing id_str", i));
        }
    }
    
    Ok((items.len(), warnings))
}
```

### 3. Duplicate ID Detection

```rust
fn check_duplicate_ids(archive_path: &Path) -> HealthCheck {
    let mut seen_ids: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
    let mut duplicates: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    
    // Load all tweet IDs from archive
    if let Ok(tweets) = parse_tweets_file(archive_path) {
        for tweet in tweets {
            if !seen_ids.insert(tweet.id.clone()) {
                duplicates.push(tweet.id);
            }
        }
    }
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Archive,
        name: "Duplicate Tweet IDs".into(),
        status: if duplicates.is_empty() { CheckStatus::Pass } else { CheckStatus::Warning },
        message: if duplicates.is_empty() {
            "No duplicates detected".into()
        } else {
            format!("{} duplicate IDs found", duplicates.len())
        },
        suggestion: if !duplicates.is_empty() {
            Some(format!("Duplicate IDs: {}...", duplicates[..3.min(duplicates.len())].join(", ")))
        } else {
            None
        },
    }
}
```

### 4. Timestamp Consistency

```rust
fn check_timestamp_consistency(archive_path: &Path) -> HealthCheck {
    let mut issues = Vec::new();
    
    if let Ok(tweets) = parse_tweets_file(archive_path) {
        for tweet in &tweets {
            // Check for future dates
            if tweet.created_at > Utc::now() {
                issues.push(format!("{}: future date", tweet.id));
            }
            // Check for impossibly old dates (before Twitter existed)
            if tweet.created_at.year() < 2006 {
                issues.push(format!("{}: before 2006", tweet.id));
            }
        }
    }
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Archive,
        name: "Timestamp Validity".into(),
        status: if issues.is_empty() { CheckStatus::Pass } else { CheckStatus::Warning },
        message: if issues.is_empty() {
            "All timestamps valid".into()
        } else {
            format!("{} timestamp issues", issues.len())
        },
        suggestion: None,
    }
}
```

## Logging

- Log each file being checked with tracing::debug!
- Log parse errors with tracing::warn!
- Log timing for large files with tracing::info!

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Required files check works
- [ ] Optional files don't cause errors
- [ ] JSON parsing validates structure
- [ ] Duplicate detection scans all tweets
- [ ] Timestamp validation catches anomalies
- [ ] All checks return HealthCheck structs
- [ ] Performance < 5s for 100k tweet archive","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFox","created_at":"2026-01-10T19:09:35.993651-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:19:26.23636315-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:19:26.23636315-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-11.4.1","depends_on_id":"xf-11.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-11.4.2","title":"Implement Tantivy index health check","description":"## Goal

Implement Tantivy index health checks to verify the search index is valid and performant.

## Checks to Implement

### 1. Index Directory Existence

```rust
fn check_index_directory(index_path: &Path) -> HealthCheck {
    if !index_path.exists() {
        return HealthCheck {
            category: CheckCategory::Index,
            name: "Index Directory".into(),
            status: CheckStatus::Error,
            message: format!("Index not found at {:?}", index_path),
            suggestion: Some("Run 'xf index' to create the index".into()),
        };
    }
    
    // Check for required Tantivy files
    let meta_path = index_path.join("meta.json");
    if !meta_path.exists() {
        return HealthCheck {
            category: CheckCategory::Index,
            name: "Index Directory".into(),
            status: CheckStatus::Error,
            message: "Missing meta.json - index may be corrupted".into(),
            suggestion: Some("Run 'xf reindex' to rebuild".into()),
        };
    }
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Index,
        name: "Index Directory".into(),
        status: CheckStatus::Pass,
        message: format!("Found at {:?}", index_path),
        suggestion: None,
    }
}
```

### 2. Index Version Compatibility

```rust
fn check_index_version(index: &TantivyIndex) -> HealthCheck {
    let meta = index.load_metas()?;
    let index_version = meta.index_version();
    let current_version = tantivy::version();
    
    let compatible = is_version_compatible(index_version, current_version);
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Index,
        name: "Index Version".into(),
        status: if compatible { CheckStatus::Pass } else { CheckStatus::Warning },
        message: format!("Index v{} (current: v{})", index_version, current_version),
        suggestion: if !compatible {
            Some("Consider 'xf reindex' for latest format".into())
        } else {
            None
        },
    }
}
```

### 3. Segment Health

```rust
fn check_segments(index: &TantivyIndex) -> HealthCheck {
    let reader = index.reader()?;
    let searcher = reader.searcher();
    let segment_count = searcher.segment_readers().len();
    
    let status = match segment_count {
        0 => CheckStatus::Warning,
        1..=10 => CheckStatus::Pass,
        11..=50 => CheckStatus::Warning,
        _ => CheckStatus::Warning,
    };
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Index,
        name: "Segment Count".into(),
        status,
        message: format!("{} segments", segment_count),
        suggestion: if segment_count > 10 {
            Some("Run 'xf optimize' to merge segments".into())
        } else {
            None
        },
    }
}
```

### 4. Document Count Verification

```rust
fn check_document_count(index: &TantivyIndex, storage: &Storage) -> HealthCheck {
    let reader = index.reader()?;
    let searcher = reader.searcher();
    let index_count = searcher.num_docs() as i64;
    
    // Get expected count from database
    let db_count: i64 = storage.conn
        .query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tweets", [], |r| r.get(0))?;
    
    let diff = (index_count - db_count).abs();
    let percentage_diff = if db_count > 0 {
        (diff as f64 / db_count as f64) * 100.0
    } else {
        0.0
    };
    
    let status = if diff == 0 {
        CheckStatus::Pass
    } else if percentage_diff < 1.0 {
        CheckStatus::Warning
    } else {
        CheckStatus::Error
    };
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Index,
        name: "Document Count".into(),
        status,
        message: format!("Index: {}, DB: {} (diff: {})", index_count, db_count, diff),
        suggestion: if diff > 0 {
            Some("Run 'xf reindex' to sync".into())
        } else {
            None
        },
    }
}
```

### 5. Sample Query Test

```rust
fn check_sample_query(index: &TantivyIndex) -> HealthCheck {
    let start = Instant::now();
    
    let result = index.search("test", 1);  // Simple query, limit 1
    
    let duration = start.elapsed();
    let duration_ms = duration.as_secs_f64() * 1000.0;
    
    match result {
        Ok(_) => {
            let status = if duration_ms < 10.0 {
                CheckStatus::Pass
            } else if duration_ms < 100.0 {
                CheckStatus::Warning
            } else {
                CheckStatus::Warning
            };
            
            HealthCheck {
                category: CheckCategory::Index,
                name: "Sample Query".into(),
                status,
                message: format!("{:.1}ms", duration_ms),
                suggestion: if duration_ms > 10.0 {
                    Some("Consider 'xf optimize' for faster queries".into())
                } else {
                    None
                },
            }
        }
        Err(e) => HealthCheck {
            category: CheckCategory::Index,
            name: "Sample Query".into(),
            status: CheckStatus::Error,
            message: format!("Query failed: {}", e),
            suggestion: Some("Index may be corrupted. Try 'xf reindex'".into()),
        },
    }
}
```

### 6. Index Size Check

```rust
fn check_index_size(index_path: &Path) -> HealthCheck {
    let size_bytes = calculate_directory_size(index_path)?;
    let size_mb = size_bytes as f64 / (1024.0 * 1024.0);
    
    HealthCheck {
        category: CheckCategory::Index,
        name: "Index Size".into(),
        status: CheckStatus::Pass,  // Informational
        message: format!("{:.1} MB", size_mb),
        suggestion: if size_mb > 500.0 {
            Some("Large index. Consider 'xf optimize' to reduce size".into())
        } else {
            None
        },
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Index directory check works
- [ ] Version compatibility check implemented
- [ ] Segment count reported with warnings
- [ ] Document count compared to database
- [ ] Sample query executes and times
- [ ] Index size reported
- [ ] All checks return HealthCheck structs
- [ ] Graceful handling of corrupted indexes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T19:09:36.518068-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:16:18.578901895-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:16:18.578901895-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-11.4.2","depends_on_id":"xf-11.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"xf-11.4.5","title":"Add doctor CLI subcommand with fix suggestions","description":"## Goal

Wire `xf doctor` into the CLI with clear output, stable JSON, actionable suggestions, and **safe** optional auto-fixes.

## CLI Surface

- Command: `xf doctor`
- Flags:
  - `--archive <path>` (optional; overrides config)
  - `--fix` (apply safe, idempotent repairs only)
  - `--format <text|json|json-pretty>` (reuse global format)

## Behavior & Flow

1. **Resolve archive path**
   - Precedence: `--archive` > config default (if stored) > none.
   - If none, **skip archive checks** and emit a warning + suggestion (do not fail).
2. **Run checks**
   - Archive checks: `check_required_files`, `check_json_structure`, `check_duplicate_ids`, `check_timestamp_consistency`.
   - Database checks: `Storage::database_health_checks()` (integrity, FTS, orphans, table counts).
   - Index checks: `SearchEngine::index_health_checks(&Storage)`.
   - Performance checks: `run_performance_benchmarks`.
3. **Aggregate results**
   - Stable ordering: category order (Archive → Database → Index → Performance), then name.
   - Summary counts: pass/warn/error/critical + total.
4. **Output**
   - Text: section headers, per-check emoji, summary line, then suggestions list.
   - JSON: stable schema (see below), no color.
5. **Exit codes**
   - `0` = no errors/critical
   - `1` = errors (data issues)
   - `2` = critical/corruption detected

## JSON Schema (stable)

```json
{
  "checks": [
    {"category":"archive","name":"...","status":"pass|warning|error","message":"...","suggestion":null}
  ],
  "summary": {"passed": 0, "warnings": 0, "errors": 0, "critical": 0, "total": 0},
  "suggestions": ["..."],
  "runtime_ms": 0
}
```

## --fix Behavior (safe + idempotent only)

- **Never delete** archive, DB, or index files.
- Allowed repairs (if check indicates need):
  - SQLite: `PRAGMA optimize`.
  - FTS: rebuild FTS tables **only** if orphaned/invalid (new `Storage::rebuild_fts()` if needed).
  - Tantivy: `SearchEngine::optimize()` if segment count is high (no data loss).
- Each repair emits a `HealthCheck` entry with status `Pass/Warning` and a `message` showing the action taken.
- Failures in fixes are reported with `status=Error` but **do not abort** remaining checks.

## Tests

### Unit
- Aggregation/sorting order is deterministic.
- Summary counts match check list.
- `--fix` only runs for specific warning/error states.
- JSON schema serialization (snapshot-style) and `suggestions` content.

### Integration
- `xf doctor` without archive path skips archive checks with warning.
- `xf doctor --format json` returns schema and valid JSON.
- Exit codes reflect simulated errors/critical.
- `--fix` toggles repair functions (use spies/mocks or test DBs).

### E2E Script (tests/e2e/doctor_cli.sh)
- Logs command, duration, exit code, and validates section headings.
- Validates JSON output with `jq` and required keys.
- Runs `--fix` on a known-bad DB and asserts reduced warnings.

## Logging Requirements

- `info!` for start/end of each category.
- `debug!` for per-check details and timings.
- `warn!` for skipped categories and failed repairs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `xf doctor` produces text + JSON output with stable schema
- [ ] `--fix` only performs safe, idempotent repairs
- [ ] Exit codes match summary severity
- [ ] Unit + integration + E2E tests with detailed logs
- [ ] No destructive operations or data loss

-{"id":"xf-11.4.6","title":"Add tests for doctor command","description":"## Goal

Comprehensive test coverage for `xf doctor` with deterministic fixtures and **actionable logging**.

## Unit Tests (doctor.rs + helpers)

### Archive Checks
- Required file presence detection.
- JS‑wrapped JSON validity (window.YTD prefix stripping).
- Duplicate IDs detection (warn, not crash).
- Timestamp sanity (detect obviously bad timestamps).

### Database Checks
- `PRAGMA integrity_check` handling.
- FTS5 integrity / orphan detection.
- Table count mismatches (warn vs error).

### Index Checks
- Tantivy open/segment counts and doc counts.
- Sample query latency check (warn if slow).

### Performance Checks
- Overall doctor runtime threshold on fixture data.
- Each check returns `Pass/Warning/Error` and includes a suggestion when fixable.

## Integration Tests

- `xf doctor` text output includes all sections + summary.
- `xf doctor --format json` validates stable schema.
- Exit codes: 0 for pass/warn, 1 for error, 2 for critical.

## E2E Script (tests/e2e/doctor_test.sh)

- Runs against a **known fixture** archive/DB/index (healthy) and a purposely broken fixture.
- Validates section headers, summary counts, and JSON schema via `jq`.
- Logs timestamp, command, stdout/stderr, exit code, and duration.

## Logging Requirements

- `debug!` for check start/end + timing.
- `info!` for check results.
- `warn!` for fixable issues and `error!` for critical problems.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests cover each check category
- [ ] Integration tests validate CLI output + schema
- [ ] E2E script validates healthy + broken fixtures
- [ ] Exit codes follow spec
- [ ] Detailed logs included for failures

 {"id":"xf-11.5","title":"Natural Language Date Filtering","description":"## Overview

Enable human‑friendly date expressions for `--since/--until` with **clear, deterministic semantics**, local‑time interpretation, and UTC comparison.

## Supported Expressions (must be documented + tested)

### Absolute
- ISO dates: `YYYY-MM-DD`, `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` (local).
- Month/year: `Jan 2023`, `January 2023`, `2024-02` → full month range.
- Quarter: `Q1 2024`, `Q4 2022` → quarter range.
- Seasons: `spring 2023`, `summer 2023`, `fall 2023`, `winter 2023` (winter spans Dec–Feb with year boundary).

### Relative / Named
- `today`, `yesterday`.
- `last|past N days/weeks/months/years`.
- `N days/weeks/months/years ago`.
- `this month`, `last month`, `this year`, `last year`.
- `weekend`, `weekdays` (range covering the most recent weekend/weekday block relative to base time).

## Semantics

- **Ranges:** convert to inclusive start/end instants (start of day 00:00:00 local, end 23:59:59 local), then store/compare in UTC.
- **Point dates:** map to start of day (00:00:00 local) unless `prefer_end` is requested.
- `--since` uses range **start**, `--until` uses range **end**.
- Error messages must include the original input and 2–3 example formats.
- `--verbose` echoes parsed range in UTC and local time for clarity.

## Test Strategy

- **Unit:** deterministic parsing via fixed base time; cover each expression type and boundary conditions (month lengths, leap years, DST transitions).
- **Integration:** CLI parsing for `--since/--until` plus verbose output; ensure ISO fallback still works.
- **E2E:** `tests/e2e/date_parse_test.sh` runs representative expressions, validates output + exit codes, logs command/stdout/stderr/duration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Natural language expressions accepted for `--since/--until`.
- [ ] ISO inputs remain supported with no regressions.
- [ ] Clear error messages with examples.
- [ ] Deterministic unit tests (no wall‑clock dependency).
- [ ] Integration + E2E tests with detailed logs.

 {"id":"xf-11.5.1","title":"Add date_parser.rs module with chrono-english","description":"## Goal

Create a new date_parser.rs module for parsing human-friendly date expressions.

## Dependencies to Add (Cargo.toml)

```toml
[dependencies]
chrono-english = "0.1"  # For natural language parsing
```

## New Module: src/date_parser.rs

### Core Types

```rust
use chrono::{DateTime, NaiveDate, NaiveTime, Utc, Local, Duration};
use chrono_english::{parse_date_string, Dialect};

/// Result of parsing a date expression
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum ParsedDate {
    /// A specific point in time
    Point(DateTime<Utc>),
    /// A date range (for expressions like "January 2023")
    Range { start: DateTime<Utc>, end: DateTime<Utc> },
}

impl ParsedDate {
    /// Get the start of this date (for --from)
    pub fn start(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {
        match self {
            Self::Point(dt) => *dt,
            Self::Range { start, .. } => *start,
        }
    }
    
    /// Get the end of this date (for --to)
    pub fn end(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {
        match self {
            Self::Point(dt) => *dt,
            Self::Range { end, .. } => *end,
        }
    }
}
```

### Main Parsing Function

```rust
/// Parse a human-readable date expression.
///
/// # Arguments
/// *  - The date string to parse
/// *  - If true, prefer end of period (for --to); otherwise start (for --from)
///
/// # Examples
/// ```
/// parse_human_date("yesterday", false)?;       // Start of yesterday
/// parse_human_date("last week", true)?;        // End of last week
/// parse_human_date("January 2023", false)?;    // 2023-01-01
/// parse_human_date("Q4 2022", true)?;          // 2022-12-31
/// ```
pub fn parse_human_date(input: &str, prefer_end: bool) -> Result<ParsedDate> {
    let input = input.trim().to_lowercase();
    
    // Try custom parsers first (quarters, seasons, months)
    if let Some(parsed) = try_parse_quarter(&input) {
        return Ok(parsed);
    }
    if let Some(parsed) = try_parse_season(&input) {
        return Ok(parsed);
    }
    if let Some(parsed) = try_parse_month_year(&input) {
        return Ok(parsed);
    }
    
    // Try chrono-english for natural language
    let now = Local::now();
    match parse_date_string(&input, now, Dialect::Us) {
        Ok(dt) => Ok(ParsedDate::Point(dt.with_timezone(&Utc))),
        Err(_) => {
            // Try relative expressions manually
            if let Some(parsed) = try_parse_relative(&input) {
                return Ok(parsed);
            }
            
            Err(anyhow\!("Could not parse date expression: '{}'", input))
        }
    }
}
```

### ISO Fallback

```rust
/// Try parsing as ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
pub fn try_parse_iso(input: &str) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>> {
    NaiveDate::parse_from_str(input, "%Y-%m-%d")
        .ok()
        .map(|d| d.and_hms_opt(0, 0, 0).unwrap().and_utc())
}

/// Unified parser: try natural language, fall back to ISO
pub fn parse_date_flexible(input: &str, prefer_end: bool) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>> {
    // Try human date first
    if let Ok(parsed) = parse_human_date(input, prefer_end) {
        return Ok(if prefer_end { parsed.end() } else { parsed.start() });
    }
    
    // Fall back to ISO
    try_parse_iso(input)
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Could not parse '{}' as date", input))
}
```

## Logging

- Log successful parses with tracing::debug\!
- Log fallback to ISO with tracing::trace\!
- Log parse failures with tracing::warn\!

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] date_parser.rs module created
- [ ] ParsedDate enum handles points and ranges
- [ ] chrono-english integrated
- [ ] ISO fallback works
- [ ] Module exports parse_human_date and parse_date_flexible
- [ ] Basic tests for common expressions
